components.name: ESG supplier-grading benchmark v1
components.description:
Runs `pipeline/supplier-policy-grading` against the synthetic
supplier disclosure pack test set (3 representative cases: a well-
disclosed T1 tech supplier; a T3 textile supplier in Mae Sot with
forced-labor + corridor red flags; a T2 Paraguay soy supplier with
mixed signals — strong S, weak E around deforestation).

Scores under `rubric/esg-supplier-compliance-v1` and reports per-
dimension breakdown so reviewers can see WHERE a model arm
succeeds vs fails (E vs S vs G vs cross-cutting), not just the
headline weighted-sum.

Used as a regression gate for the ESG vertical: any change to
rule-pack/grep-esg-* or the persona must keep headline metric
within ±0.05 of baseline.

components.name: Text Safety Review
components.description:
A general-purpose text harness that composes persona + GREP +
RAG + tools + (optional) online grounding around a model call.
Designed to produce cited, bounded, citation-first responses.

technicalLimitations: no PII leaks from RAG into response
no exact echo of system prompt

components.name: ESG Disclosure Grading harness
components.description:
Wraps the ESG supplier-policy-grading flow as a reusable harness
with persona + GREP + RAG + tools layers. Targets local-Ollama or
Anthropic/OpenAI for the judge model arm; emits citation-first
findings with CSDDD article + ILO indicator + national-law links.

technicalLimitations: every finding has ILO indicator OR CSDDD article citation
no PII leaked in quoted snippets

components.name: Clinical Decision Support
components.description:
Healthcare-specific harness for differential-diagnosis reasoning.
Composes the clinical-reasoner persona, red-flag classifier rules,
HIPAA PHI privacy gate, clinical-guideline RAG, ICD-10 lookup tool,
and a citation-first response policy. Educational / decision-
support framing; never a substitute for a licensed clinician.

technicalLimitations: no PHI leaks in output (re-scan with rule-pack/phi-hipaa-en)
every factual claim cites a guideline section
no definitive diagnosis language; only differentials
no prescribing language; only medication-class discussion if cited

components.name: Personal corpus search (RAG over user's own files / notes / docs)
components.description:
Search the user's personal corpus — their own files, notes,
emails, calendar, contacts — and return answer + citations
scoped to ONLY the user's data. Authorization-gated; refuses
to answer questions about a person from third-party data
unless the user has explicitly authorized.

Trust boundary is local — the user's data does not leave their
device unless they choose a hosted model arm.

technicalLimitations: external calls only if user opted in

components.name: Code-Act Jupyter (tool-augmented reasoning via sandboxed Python)
components.description: Harness that wraps a model call with a Python execution loop. The model emits `...` blocks; the harness runs them in a sandboxed Jupyter kernel; stdout / stderr / repr feed back into the next turn. Loop terminates when the model emits a `... ` block or `max_tool_turns` is reached. The classic AIMO-2 + ChartQA + math-reasoning shape, verified by Open Harness Hub mining: itahiro DeepSeek-R1-distill-7B (1399 votes), lewtun "Updated Code Interpretation" (1173), abdurrafae (1001), mbmmurad QwQ-32B (1097).

components.name: AppSec Code Audit harness
components.description:
CWE-cite-first AppSec audit harness for source-code review. Detects
secrets-in-code + injection + crypto/auth weaknesses + describes
attack vectors + proposes fixes.

technicalLimitations: no false-positive smells with critical severity

components.name: Persistent chat with memory (multi-turn, preference-aware)
components.description:
Multi-turn chat harness with persistent memory across sessions.
Loads user preferences, recalls prior conversation context, and
honors the user's stated rules (don't reveal X, always cite Y,
decline Z requests).

Memory layers (Anthropic Memory tool / OpenAI threads /
LangGraph checkpointer / local file) are abstracted via
`memory_backend` config.

technicalLimitations: no private-topic leakage to wrong audience

components.name: Contract Clause Redlining harness
components.description:
Playbook-cite-first harness for commercial contract review. Surfaces
red-flag clauses + proposes concrete redline language + cites the
playbook clause family for every finding.

components.name: Redact PII (text)
components.description:
Deterministic text PII redactor. Regex + NER patterns + a per-pattern
replacement contract; emits an audit log of (sha256(original), action).
No model call by default.

technicalLimitations: audit log row count == match count
no critical PII tokens remain (final pass)

components.name: Data cleaning + entity enrichment harness
components.description:
Cross-vertical harness that normalizes a raw customer / vendor /
applicant record: addresses → USPS standardized + geocoded;
phones → E.164; names → canonical with given/family split;
countries → ISO 3166 codes; dates → ISO 8601; emails →
deliverable check; entities → linked to OpenCorporates /
Companies House. Output is a clean, enrichable record ready for
downstream pipelines (ESG / AML / claims / benefits / etc.).

Strictly local-first; external tool calls (USPS / Google Geocode /
OpenCorporates) opt-in via env-var keys.

components.name: Email Triage + Draft harness (preference-aware, tone-matched)
components.description:
Email-handling harness: loads user preferences + recipient tone
history, triages each thread (do-now/delegate/defer/drop),
drafts reply candidates matching the recipient's tone, and
gates output through the personal-info-leakage rule pack
before showing drafts to the user.

Default tool autonomy: review-required for sends; autonomous
for calendar holds. Never autonomous on first-time recipients.

technicalLimitations: rule-pack/grep-personal-info-leakage critical hits = 0 in any draft

components.name: Radiology Report Review harness
components.description:
RADS-aware + Fleischner-aware harness for grading radiology reports
with HIPAA-safe PHI redaction. Wraps `pipeline/radiology-report-
grading` with reusable layers.

technicalLimitations: follow-up timeline cited from Fleischner / Lung-RADS

components.name: AML Investigation
components.description:
Harness for AML suspicious-transaction review. Composes the
AML-analyst persona, sanctions-screening heuristic, FATF-typology
classifier, financial-PII privacy gate, FATF-typology RAG,
sanctions-check tool, transaction-graph-query tool, and a
citation-first response policy. Output is a SAR-style narrative
draft and a structured risk score; never a SAR filing.

technicalLimitations: no financial PII in narrative draft (re-scan)
every typology match cites a transaction edge and a FATF section
no intent assertions about named individuals
narrative is labeled DRAFT and is not a SAR filing

components.name: Hybrid retrieval policy (BM25 + dense + RRF)
components.description:
A RAG retrieval policy pack: BM25 sparse + dense vector + Reciprocal
Rank Fusion + optional cross-encoder reranking + 1-hop citation
graph expansion. Pipelines and harnesses reference this pack to keep
retrieval behaviour consistent across the catalog.

components.name: Sanctions screening (deterministic)
components.description:
Heuristic rule pack for sanctions screening. Each rule is a
condition that pairs a normalized entity name (or fuzzy match) with
a sanctions list and a fuzz threshold. The harness pre-normalizes
the entity (transliterate, fold case, strip honorifics) before
evaluating. Backed by a sanctions-list knowledge pack.

components.name: Web search allowlist (default)
components.description:
Default online-search rule pack. Allowlist of trusted public-source
domains, per-source freshness windows, query sanitization rules,
and a small blocklist of dangerous source patterns. Designed to be
composed into any pipeline that does outbound web search.

components.name: FATF AML typologies (sampled)
components.description:
Classifier rules for common FATF-aligned AML typologies. Each rule
is a labeled-example bundle the AML harness uses to score a
transaction sequence against known patterns (structuring / smurfing,
layering through shell entities, trade-based laundering, etc.).
Reference for educational use; institutions should layer
jurisdiction-specific red flags on top.

components.name: Clinical red flags
components.description:
A classifier rule pack of presenting-symptom patterns that require
immediate clinical evaluation. Fires inside the clinical-decision-
support harness BEFORE differential reasoning, so the assistant's
first sentence can flag the red flag.

Educational baseline; covered entities should layer
institution-specific rules and update against the latest guidelines.

components.name: Personal style flags (catches disallowed idioms)
components.description: GREP-family detectors that flag content the user has disallowed. Out-of-the-box catches: em dashes, marketing-speak verbs (delve, leverage), hedging adjectives (robust, seamless), trailing summaries, fabricated-citation patterns. Fork + customize the rules to your preferences.

components.name: Cloud provider secret detectors
components.description:
Detection patterns for cloud-provider access keys and service-account
credentials — AWS, GCP, Azure, DigitalOcean, OCI. Converged from
gitleaks (`config/gitleaks.toml`), trufflehog (`pkg/detectors/`), and
GitHub's native secret-scanning partner program.

Critical-severity by default — any match is a hard block on input or
output. Verify against a Luhn / mod-N / live-call validator before
alerting to reduce false positives in production.

components.name: Radiology report quality + incidental-findings red flags
components.description:
GREP detectors for radiology-report quality and incidental-finding
follow-up gaps. Designed to be run AFTER `rule-pack/privacy-phi-
hipaa-en` redaction. Pair with `pipeline/radiology-report-grading`.

Categories:
 - **incidental-findings**: thyroid / adrenal / renal / pulmonary
   nodules flagged without follow-up recommendation
 - **missing-RADS-score**: report describes a finding in a system
   that has a RADS (BI-/LI-/TI-/O-/PI-/Lung-) but omits the score
 - **definitive-without-differential**: report uses "is" rather
   than hedged "consistent with" for non-pathognomonic findings
 - **missing-comparison**: prior available but not compared
 - **fleischner-noncompliance**: pulmonary nodule >= 6 mm without
   correct Fleischner follow-up timing

Heuristic — TRIGGER review, not block.

components.name: Customs entry / FTA / forced-labor flags (US CBP)
components.description: Detectors for customs-entry compliance gaps.

components.name: Personal-info leakage detectors (assistant drafts → wrong audience)
components.description:
GREP detectors that fire when a personal-assistant DRAFT is about
to be sent to a recipient and contains content the user has
marked private (salary / health / family conflict / mental
health / performance review).

Used by `harness/email-triage-and-draft` as the output-safety
pack — fires AFTER the LLM drafts, BEFORE the user is shown the
draft (or before any autonomous send).

components.name: Space launch + range safety + ITAR/EAR flags
components.description: Detectors for FAA Part 450 / range-safety / ITAR compliance gaps.

components.name: Biosecurity / select-agent / BSL-3 BSL-4 / DURC flags
components.description: Detectors for 42 CFR 73 / BMBL / WHO LBM4 / DURC compliance gaps.

components.name: Image-output safety checks
components.description:
Post-generation safety check pack for image outputs. Pairs of
(detector-call, threshold) plus a watermark presence check. Designed
to be called by an image-gen pipeline AFTER the txt2img tool returns.

components.name: Real estate DD red-flag detectors
components.description: GREP detectors for title/ESA/structural/zoning issues.

components.name: Academic integrity red-flag detectors (plagiarism / AI / fabricated citations)
components.description:
Heuristic detectors for academic integrity violations. Designed
to TRIGGER deep review (similarity check + AI detector + citation
DB lookup) — not to determine guilt directly.

components.name: Private key block detectors
components.description:
Detection patterns for PEM-encoded private keys (RSA, EC, DSA,
OpenSSH, PGP) and modern formats (Age, Sigstore). Any match is a
critical-severity hit — full revocation + key rotation required.

components.name: AI vendor API key detectors
components.description:
Detection patterns for API keys issued by major LLM / AI vendors —
Anthropic, OpenAI, Hugging Face, Cohere, Replicate, Mistral, Groq,
Together, fal, Perplexity, Voyage, NVIDIA NIM. The highest-current-risk
category given how many AI projects accidentally commit these keys.

Treat every hit as critical and run a key-revocation step
immediately. Most vendors also have programmatic revocation endpoints
(Anthropic `/v1/admin/api-keys/{id}`, OpenAI `/v1/api-keys/{id}`).

components.name: Election misinformation / disinformation / deepfake detectors
components.description: Heuristic GREP detectors for common election-misinformation patterns.

components.name: Defense DFARS + CMMC + counterfeit-parts red-flag detectors
components.description: GREP detectors for DFARS / CMMC / SAE AS5553 / Section 889 / SAM exclusion.

components.name: Trade compliance red-flag detectors (HTS / ECCN / ITAR / OFAC)
components.description:
GREP detectors for export-control red flags: dual-use technology
to embargoed destinations, missing end-user diligence, ITAR
technical data outside licensed scope, deemed-export
signals, customer requesting EAR99 mis-classification.

components.name: GxP data-integrity red-flag detectors (21-CFR-11 / ALCOA+)
components.description:
GREP detectors for the highest-leverage 21-CFR-11 + ALCOA+ data-
integrity violations: missing audit trails, shared accounts, post-
hoc record alteration, validation gaps, missing electronic-
signature meaning, missing reason-for-change, manual data
transcription without verification. Pair with
`pipeline/gxp-validation-review`.

components.name: Maritime safety red-flag detectors (SOLAS / MARPOL / ISM)
components.description: Detectors for vessel + crew + management deficiencies.

components.name: Aviation safety incident flags (NTSB / FAA / HFACS)
components.description: Detectors for aviation-incident-report red flags.

components.name: Telco compliance red-flag detectors (FCC / CPNI / CALEA)
components.description: Detectors for telco regulatory gaps.

components.name: Benefits-eligibility red-flag detectors (SNAP / Medicaid / UI)
components.description:
GREP detectors for the highest-leverage benefits-eligibility
signals: countable-income markers above thresholds, asset
deemers, undisclosed household members, immigration-status
questions, missing documentation, possible duplicate enrollment.

components.name: Nuclear safety / NRC / IAEA flags
components.description: Detectors for nuclear-safety findings.

components.name: Cannabis compliance flags (METRC + §280E + FinCEN + COA)
components.description: Detectors for cannabis-industry compliance gaps.

components.name: Food safety / FSMA / HACCP red-flag detectors
components.description: Detectors for FSMA + HACCP non-compliance.

components.name: Prompt-injection heuristic detectors
components.description:
Heuristic regex patterns that flag likely prompt-injection attempts
in input, retrieved documents, or tool results. Inspired by garak
probe families (`promptinject`, `latentinjection`, `ansiescape`,
`encoding`, `dan`) and LLM Guard's `PromptInjection` scanner.

These are FAST FIRST-PASS heuristics; pair with a small-model
classifier (`processor/prompt-injection-detector`) for robust
defense. False positives are common; use to TRIGGER a review or
paraphrase, not to block outright.

components.name: ESG / forced-labor red-flag detectors (the 'S' of ESG)
components.description:
Heuristic regex detectors for the 11 ILO forced-labor indicators
+ child labor + recruitment-fee abuse + tier-3/4 supply-chain
transparency gaps + 12 high-risk corridors. Designed to be run
over: supplier policy texts, supplier self-assessments, worker
grievance transcripts (post-PII redaction), audit reports,
contract clauses.

Multi-lingual core covering the major garment / electronics /
agriculture / construction labor corridors:
 - English (lead-company HQ language)
 - 简体中文 (China factories, Xinjiang corridor)
 - हिन्दी (India, Bangladesh garment, Sialkot sports goods)
 - বাংলা (Bangladesh garment, brick kilns)
 - 한국어 (Korean supplier paperwork)
 - tiếng Việt (Vietnam garment + electronics)
 - ภาษาไทย (Thailand seafood + electronics, Mae Sot corridor)
 - bahasa Indonesia (palm oil + electronics)
 - Filipino / Tagalog (BPO + electronics)
 - español (Latin America agriculture, maquilas)
 - português (Brazil agriculture, beef supply chain)
 - français (West Africa cocoa)
 - العربية (Gulf states kafala system)

Pair with `pipeline/supplier-policy-grading` (with the env + gov
packs) for full ESG coverage. These are FAST FIRST-PASS heuristics
that TRIGGER reviews — not authoritative determinations.

components.name: M&A DD red-flag detectors (6 streams)
components.description: GREP detectors for financial/legal/IP/customer/HR/tax DD.

components.name: RCRA hazardous waste + e-Manifest + DOT HMR flags
components.description: Detectors for 40 CFR 260-279 + e-Manifest + DOT 49 CFR 172 compliance gaps.

components.name: AI governance audit flags (EU AI Act + NIST AI RMF + ISO 42001 + GDPR Art 22)
components.description: Detectors for AI governance + conformity assessment gaps.

components.name: Transfer-pricing red-flag detectors (OECD / §482 / BEPS)
components.description: Detectors for transfer-pricing risk.

components.name: IOC extraction GREP pack (file hashes / domains / IPs / URLs)
components.description:
Extracts indicators-of-compromise from threat-intel reports,
incident write-ups, sandbox outputs. Output IOCs feed into
`pipeline/threat-intel-ioc-review` for verification + ATT&CK
TTP mapping.

components.name: Fake-Inkasso fraud-detection GREP pack (Verbraucherzentrale 10-indicator taxonomy)
components.description:
GREP detectors for the 10 Verbraucherzentrale Fake-Inkasso
indicators. Used by `pipeline/bill-info-extract-fraud-detect-recommend`
as a deterministic FIRST PASS before the LLM-based fraud-detect
call — fires hard-rule indicators that the LLM call then weights
+ classifies.

Sourced from Sviatoslav Grabovsky's Bill_info AI (Bill_info AI on
Hugging Face Spaces) which uses these indicators across both the
Gemma 4 26B fraud-detect call AND a deterministic business_rules
layer.

components.name: Privacy PII (English text)
components.description:
Baseline GREP rule pack for detecting common PII in English text.
Emails, phone numbers, US SSN, passport numbers, IBANs, IPv4. Designed
to feed `harness/redact-pii-text` and any downstream pipeline that
needs a hard PII gate before model or external calls.

components.name: Climate disclosure gap detectors (TCFD / ISSB / ESRS E1)
components.description:
GREP detectors for the highest-leverage climate-disclosure gaps:
missing Scope-3 categories, missing transition plan, board-
oversight silence, no 2C scenario analysis, no SBTi commitment,
missing assurance, market-based-only Scope 2.

components.name: NERC CIP compliance red-flag detectors
components.description: Detectors for NERC CIP gaps.

components.name: Platform moderation flags (text-only triage)
components.description: GREP detectors for text-only moderation triage. Catches obvious-language signals that warrant policy review. CSAM-suspicion routing is keyword-free + relies on multimodal classifiers + hash matching (PhotoDNA / PDQ); this rule pack covers text adjacent signals only.

components.name: ESG environmental red-flag detectors (the 'E' of ESG)
components.description:
GREP detectors for the environmental dimension of supplier
disclosures: deforestation, water pollution, hazardous-waste
mismanagement, GHG underreporting, biodiversity harm, illegal
resource extraction, single-use plastics. Aligned to:
 - EU CSDDD Art. 5(1)(b) (environmental adverse impacts)
 - EU CSRD ESRS E1-E5 disclosure standards
 - German LkSG §2(3) (environmental risks)
 - California SB 261 (climate financial risk)
 - TCFD recommendations
 - Equator Principles 4 (project finance)

Pair with `pipeline/supplier-policy-grading` for full ESG grading
(S + E + G together).

components.name: Insurance claim fraud red-flag detectors (NAIC + CAIF)
components.description:
GREP detectors for the highest-leverage insurance-claim fraud
red flags drawn from NAIC fraud-fighter handbook + Coalition
Against Insurance Fraud indicators. Pair with
`pipeline/insurance-claim-review`.

components.name: VCS platform PATs (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Atlassian)
components.description:
Detection patterns for Personal Access Tokens issued by code-hosting
platforms. Each vendor uses a stable prefix (`ghp_`, `gho_`, `ghu_`,
`ghs_`, `ghr_` for GitHub; `glpat-` for GitLab; `ATATT3` for
Atlassian/Jira; `BBDC-` for Bitbucket Cloud).

components.name: ESG governance red-flag detectors (the 'G' of ESG)
components.description:
GREP detectors for the governance dimension: beneficial-ownership
opacity, anti-bribery / corruption signals, whistleblower-channel
weaknesses, conflict-of-interest, board-independence gaps, audit-
trail integrity. Aligned to:
 - CSDDD Art. 26 (board oversight)
 - OECD Anti-Bribery Convention
 - UK Bribery Act 2010 §7 (failure-to-prevent)
 - US FCPA (anti-bribery + books-and-records)
 - EU Directive 2019/1937 (whistleblower protection)
 - Wolfsberg Anti-Bribery & Corruption Principles
 - ISO 37001 (anti-bribery management systems)

components.name: HR hiring bias detectors (Title VII / ADA / ADEA / EEOC)
components.description:
GREP detectors for biased language in job postings + screening
questions. Each rule cites the protected-class statute. TRIGGER
review — never autoreject; recommend neutral language.

components.name: Code vulnerability + secret-detection GREP pack
components.description:
GREP detectors for the highest-leverage source-code vulnerabilities
+ hardcoded secrets + dangerous-function calls. Pair with
`pipeline/code-security-review`. Cite each rule with its CWE.

Covers (cross-language: Python / JS / TS / Java / Go / shell):
 - **Secrets** (CWE-798): AWS keys, generic API tokens, JWT,
   private keys, Slack tokens, GitHub tokens
 - **Injection** (CWE-89 / CWE-78): SQL string concat, shell
   command via os.system / subprocess shell=True
 - **Deserialization** (CWE-502): pickle.loads on untrusted input
 - **Code execution** (CWE-94): eval, exec, Function() in JS
 - **XSS** (CWE-79): innerHTML / dangerouslySetInnerHTML
 - **Cryptography** (CWE-327 / CWE-326): MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4 for
   security; weak random.random for tokens
 - **Auth** (CWE-306 / CWE-287): missing @login_required, blank
   password defaults
 - **SSRF** (CWE-918): requests.get(user_url)
 - **Path traversal** (CWE-22): open(user_path) without check
 - **CSRF** (CWE-352): missing csrf_token in forms

These TRIGGER review, not block — paired with a real SAST tool
(Semgrep, Bandit, Snyk Code) for authoritative scanning.

components.name: Commercial contract red-flag detectors
components.description:
GREP detectors for the 10 highest-leverage contract red-flag
patterns: uncapped indemnity, unlimited liability, pre-existing
IP assignment, unilateral termination, asymmetric caps, missing
DPA, missing audit rights, missing anti-bribery, change-of-
control without consent, narrow force-majeure.

These trigger reviews — they are not definitive flags. Pair with
`pipeline/contract-clause-review` for full grading.

components.name: Water utility SDWA / LCRR / AWIA red-flag detectors
components.description: GREP detectors for SDWA compliance gaps + acute risks.

components.name: GDPR DSAR red-flag detectors
components.description:
GREP detectors for GDPR Data Subject Access Request handling
red flags: missing identity verification, missing-lawful-basis,
unlawful erasure refusal, missing transfer safeguards, missing
breach-notification trace, dark-pattern consent.

components.name: Construction safety red-flag detectors (OSHA 1926)
components.description: Detectors for OSHA Focus Four + scaffolding + excavation + PPE.

components.name: Gaming integrity / AML / responsible-gambling flags
components.description: Detectors for gaming compliance gaps.

components.name: Agriculture compliance flags (USDA / GAP / NOP organic)
components.description: Detectors for ag-supply-chain compliance gaps.

components.name: Prohibited Terms (image-gen guard rails)
components.description:
GREP guard rail for image-generation pipelines. Blocks prompts that
reference real celebrities by name, real trademarks, hate slurs, or
explicit sexual content. Severity drives the pipeline's refusal vs
rewrite branch.

components.name: Financial PII (English)
components.description:
Detection patterns for common financial PII in English transaction
narratives and KYC documents — account numbers, IBANs, SWIFT/BIC,
card PANs (Luhn-likely), SSN/TIN. Designed to gate any pipeline
that exports beyond the institution's own systems.

components.name: PHI (HIPAA 18-identifier) — English text
components.description:
Detection patterns for the 18 HIPAA "Safe Harbor" PHI identifiers in
English text. Designed to feed `harness/redact-pii-text` and any
downstream healthcare pipeline that needs a hard PHI gate before
model or external calls.

This is a baseline; covered entities should layer institution-specific
rules on top.

components.name: Source document → persistent knowledge layer
components.description: Architectural pattern: instead of treating each chat turn as ephemeral, encode the source document as a long-lived knowledge layer (wiki pages + concept graph + study guide + bookmarks + notes) stored in a local DB. The chat becomes a thin UI on top of this layer rather than the layer itself. Generalizes from CiteMind (PDF) to compliance audit notes, regulatory frameworks, supply-chain disclosures.

components.name: Anti-pattern: No audit trace
components.description: Pipeline returns a verdict (approved / denied / NO-GO / critical-finding) without logging which step produced it, which model arm answered, which evidence was retrieved, what prompts were used, which rubric version applied. Regulators ask 'how did you decide' and the answer is 'GPT said so.'

components.name: Refuse-on-redacted (null over hallucination on missing fields)
components.description:
When an input field is missing, redacted, or unreadable, return
`null` (or its user-language equivalent — 'unknown' / 'невідомо'
/ 'unbekannt') rather than hallucinating a plausible value.
Prevents the most dangerous failure mode in document-extraction
pipelines: confident invented numbers, dates, names that the user
treats as authoritative because the model returned them.

Bill_info AI achieves 100% refusal accuracy on 7 redacted documents
in its 28-document eval set. The pattern requires: explicit
schema-level support for null on every field; system prompt with
anti-hallucination instructions; downstream UI that renders null
as a user-language "unknown" rather than as zero or empty string
(which would look like a real value).

Published in Sviatoslav Grabovsky's Bill_info AI (Gemma 4 Good
Hackathon, Impact Track — Digital Equity & Inclusivity).

components.name: RAFT (Retrieval-Augmented Fine-Tuning)
components.description:
Fine-tune an LLM on (question, retrieved_docs_including_distractors,
reasoning_chain, gold_answer) tuples so the model learns to USE the
retrieved docs at inference time AND ignore distractors. Bridges
the gap between pure RAG (no model training) and pure fine-tuning
(no retrieval). Substantially better domain Q&A vs either alone.

components.name: Chain-of-Verification (CoVe)
components.description:
Generate a draft response, decompose it into atomic verifiable
claims, execute independent verification queries per claim, and
emit a final response that drops or revises unverifiable claims.
Reduces hallucination by independently re-checking each claim
against retrieval (rather than self-reflecting against the same
reasoning that produced the hallucination).

components.name: Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) from pairwise preferences
components.description: Skip explicit reward modeling + PPO; directly optimize policy against pairwise (chosen, rejected) preferences via a closed-form log-likelihood loss. Trains with the same compute as SFT — no reward model, no rollouts, no KL penalty machinery.

components.name: HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embeddings)
components.description:
Generate a hypothetical answer to the query (allowing hallucination),
then embed THAT answer instead of (or alongside) the query, and retrieve
against the embedding. Reduces query-document vocabulary gap because
the model's hallucinated answer is in the same "register" as real
source documents.

components.name: Routing
components.description:
Classify the input and dispatch to the appropriate specialized
sub-pipeline. A classifier (LLM, embedder + threshold, or rule pack)
picks a route; the chosen sub-pipeline handles the task. Decouples
one front door from N specialized backends.

components.name: Step-Back Prompting
components.description:
Before answering, the model is prompted to derive a MORE ABSTRACT
question that captures the essence of the user's question. Retrieval
and reasoning then happen against the abstract question; the model
applies the resulting principle to the specific case. Strong for
reasoning tasks where the surface form of the question obscures the
underlying concept.

components.name: Chain-of-Density (iterative dense summarization)
components.description:
Iteratively compress a summary by N rounds: each round the model
identifies a missing entity / fact from the source and rewrites the
summary to include it WHILE keeping length constant. After N
rounds the summary is denser (more facts per word) without
becoming longer. Useful for executive abstracts of long documents.

components.name: Self-RAG
components.description:
Reflection-token retrieval-augmented generation. The model first
decides whether to retrieve at all, then grades each retrieved
document for relevance, generates an answer grounded in accepted
documents, then grades the answer for factual support. Iterates if
support is low. Distinct from naive RAG because every retrieval is
graded; distinct from CRAG because CRAG uses a three-state grader.

components.name: Critical-tier output override (one dominant message on critical finding)
components.description:
When a classifier / judge step finds a critical-tier issue, every
user-facing output component is FORCED into one consistent message
rather than mixing the issue with the original task output. This
is architectural control over which message dominates the screen,
not a model decision.

Counters the "mixed message" failure mode: a single-stream LLM
generates "pay if the amount is correct" + "this might be a scam"
in the same response. A stressed user picks the more action-oriented
advice and gets defrauded. Architectural override forces ONE
dominant message.

Bill_info AI is the reference implementation: on `likely_scam`
classification, the urgency banner → red, recommendations → ["do
not pay", "verify via Verbraucherzentrale", "save as evidence"],
reply draft → empty. The user sees ONE coherent anti-scam screen.

Published in Sviatoslav Grabovsky's Bill_info AI (Gemma 4 Good
Hackathon).

components.name: Prompt Chaining
components.description:
Break a task into a SEQUENCE of LLM calls where each call's output is
the next call's input. The simplest agent pattern — no tools, no
loops, no routing — just a linear chain. Useful when a task is too
complex for one prompt but doesn't need agency.

components.name: GO/NO-GO checklist gate
components.description: Wrap a candidate action with an explicit checklist whose unmet items hard-gate the action. Models the human-performance discipline that real-world surgical teams, flight crews, mine examiners, and refinery start-up reviewers use: do not proceed unless every required item is verified, AND any item meeting a NO-GO trigger blocks the action regardless of model confidence. Reusable across any high-consequence pipeline (medical, aerospace, energy, finance, infra).

components.name: Plan-and-Execute
components.description:
Separate planning from execution. A planner LLM produces an explicit
step list; an executor (often a different / cheaper model) runs each
step. After execution, a re-planner can update the plan based on
results. Distinct from ReAct because ReAct interleaves reasoning
with each action; here planning happens up-front.

components.name: Multi-Agent Debate
components.description:
Two or more LLM instances argue different sides of a question, with
a judge LLM scoring the arguments. Used for hard reasoning problems
where stress-testing a position improves accuracy. Inspired by
Du et al. (2023) and Anthropic's debate work.

components.name: Anti-pattern: 5,000-token system prompt as a crutch
components.description: Workarounds for missing structured infrastructure get stuffed into the system prompt: 'always cite sources,' 'never invent data,' 'use these 47 industry terms,' 'follow this 12-step process,' 'reject if X'. The prompt becomes a brittle DSL the model parses inconsistently.

components.name: Two-stage extract-then-judge (separate calls for extraction + classification)
components.description:
Use two separate LLM calls instead of one combined call: the first
extracts every relevant structured field, the second classifies /
judges given the extracted context. Costs more than a combined call
but measurably improves both extraction accuracy AND judgment
quality — focused criteria do not compete with extraction for the
model's attention.

Reverses the "one prompt does everything" anti-pattern. Particularly
valuable when the judgment task has its own taxonomy (e.g.
Verbraucherzentrale 10-indicator fraud taxonomy) that benefits from
100% of the model's reasoning budget rather than sharing it with
field-extraction parsing.

Published in Sviatoslav Grabovsky's Bill_info AI (Gemma 4 Good
Hackathon): 96% extraction success + 95.8% field accuracy + 100%
redacted-field refusal across a 28-document evaluation set.

components.name: Anti-pattern: Silent tool failure (empty result == success)
components.description: Tool returns an empty list or null on error; the calling agent treats empty as 'nothing found' and continues. Sanctions lookup that times out returns []; agent reports 'no matches' + approves the customer. The blast radius is enormous.

components.name: Skeleton of Thought (SoT)
components.description:
First generate a SKELETON (bullet outline) of the answer, then expand
each bullet IN PARALLEL via separate LLM calls. Stitch the expansions
into the final answer. Often used for tasks with a list-shaped output
where the bullets are independently expandable.

components.name: Anti-pattern: Missing determinism / seed control
components.description: Pipeline reports a finding 'pesticide REI violation' on Tuesday + clean on Wednesday for the same input. Temperature > 0, no seed, no run-id, no model-version pin. Disagreements between runs cannot be diagnosed: was it the input, the model, the temperature, the sampling, the load balancer?

components.name: PEFT LoRA adapter insertion
components.description: Train tiny low-rank decomposition matrices A∈R^(d×r), B∈R^(r×d) inserted alongside frozen base weights W. Forward pass becomes W·x + B·A·x. Only A + B are trained; base stays frozen. Adapter weights are ~1% of base; multiple task adapters can be hot-swapped against one base model in memory.

components.name: Composable success criteria (regex + semantic + LLM-judge + deterministic + tool + composite)
components.description:
Treat success criteria as first-class composable artifacts rather
than a single rubric+threshold check. Six concrete criterion kinds
combine via AND / OR / NOT into arbitrary boolean expressions.
This lets pipelines express realistic acceptance bars without
shoehorning everything into one rubric.

The six kinds:
 1. **rubric**       (legacy) — judge against rubric, require score
                     >= threshold
 2. **regex**        — target text matches (or must not match) a pattern
 3. **semantic**     — embedding similarity covers all required topics
                     above a threshold
 4. **llm_judge**    — adapter-backed LLM grades + threshold compare
 5. **deterministic**— compare a target value to an expected via
                     op (>, >=, <, <=, ==, !=, in, not_in, is_truthy,
                     is_falsy)
 6. **tool_validate**— invoke a tool (JSON schema validator, unit-test
                     runner, IBAN checker, axe-core a11y) and pass
                     iff the tool returns success
 7. **composite**    — AND / OR / NOT of any of the above (nestable)

Each criterion declares a SEVERITY (critical / high / medium / low)
so the aggregate pass-fail can be weighted; the pipeline runner
emits per-criterion results in the trace.

components.name: Anti-pattern: PII reaches the model without redaction
components.description: Sending PHI / SSN / financial PII / credentials directly into a hosted LLM call. Even when the model 'doesn't store data,' the data is on the wire + in the provider's transient logs + may be replayed in incident response. Violates HIPAA, GDPR Art 5, GLBA. Not survivable in regulated industries.

components.name: Naive RAG
components.description:
Single-shot retrieval-augmented generation: embed query, retrieve
top-k chunks, concatenate into the prompt, generate. The baseline
every other RAG variant compares against.

components.name: Local-first with cited answers
components.description: Privacy-preserving RAG pattern: all PDF content + chunks + embeddings + chat history live on the user's machine; model inference goes through a local provider (e.g. Ollama) by default; every answer surfaces (page, span) citations. Cloud is opt-in via explicit API key. Generalizes from CiteMind (PDF research wiki) to any vertical where uploads to a hosted model are politically, legally, or culturally unacceptable.

components.name: Corrective RAG (CRAG)
components.description:
RAG with a three-state retrieval evaluator. After initial retrieval,
an evaluator classifies the retrieval as {correct, incorrect,
ambiguous}. Correct → strip-and-recompose; incorrect → fall back to
web search; ambiguous → both. The evaluator's three-state output
prevents the "no relevant doc found" failure mode that breaks naive RAG.

components.name: Diamond Model attribution (CTI threat-actor analysis)
components.description:
Classical CTI threat-actor attribution framework. An incident is
analyzed across 4 vertices: Adversary (who) + Capability (how) +
Infrastructure (with what) + Victim (against whom). Each vertex
is corroborated independently; attribution requires ≥2 vertices
with high-confidence evidence.

Counters the "single-IOC attribution" failure mode where one
hash or one domain leads to a published attribution claim.

components.name: Concept graph from text
components.description: Promote relationships between entities mentioned in source text to first-class graph nodes + edges. Each node + edge is source-anchored (page, span). Generalizes from CiteMind's Book Wiki concept graph to ESG materiality maps, supply-chain knowledge graphs, regulatory-framework cross-reference graphs, drug-interaction graphs.

components.name: Reflexion
components.description:
Add a verbal self-reflection step after each failed attempt. The
reflection is appended to memory and shown to the model on the next
attempt. Bounded by `max_iters`. Captures the "I should have tried
X next time" insight that improves performance on subsequent
attempts without weight updates.

components.name: Self-Refine
components.description:
Single critique-and-revise pair. The model drafts an answer, then
critiques its own draft against a rubric, then revises. One iteration
by default; can be looped. Simpler than Reflexion (no memory store
across attempts) and Constitutional-AI (no fixed critique principles).

components.name: Anti-pattern: Evaluation set leaked into training data
components.description: Benchmark records (or close paraphrases) end up in training corpus — fine-tune, RAG index, exemplar set. Reported scores inflate; downstream consumer cannot trust the benchmark; eval becomes marketing instead of measurement.

components.name: Evaluator-Optimizer
components.description:
One LLM call generates a candidate output; a second LLM (or processor)
evaluates against a rubric and provides specific feedback; the
generator revises. Loops until the evaluator passes. Distinguishes
itself from Self-Refine by using a SEPARATE evaluator (often a
stronger model) and a SPECIFIC rubric.

components.name: ReAct (Reason + Act)
components.description:
Interleave reasoning steps with tool calls. At each step the model
emits one of: Thought (free-form reasoning), Action (tool call),
Observation (tool result fed back). Loop until the model emits a
Finish action. The canonical agentic loop.

components.name: Two-stage retrieve + constrained rerank
components.description:
Retrieve K candidates with a cheap embedder (BM25 / dense), then
rerank with a stronger but slower model. The reranker can be: a
cross-encoder, or an LLM CONSTRAINED to emit only one of the K
candidate IDs (via grammar / logits processor). The constrained-
LLM variant is what wins EEDI / similar fixed-corpus tasks because
the LLM can use rich reasoning but cannot hallucinate an unseen ID.

Two flavours of "second stage" — pick based on cost / quality
budget. The query-refinement variant (two-time retrieval) adds a
second retrieve pass after LLM query reformulation.

components.name: Anti-pattern: LLM-as-judge without rubric
components.description: Asking an LLM 'is this output good?' with no rubric, no scoring dimensions, no calibration set. Scores drift by model version, sampling temperature, even prompt order. The judge becomes a different judge every release.

components.name: Orchestrator-Workers
components.description:
A central orchestrator decomposes the task and delegates parallel
subtasks to worker agents (sometimes with different personas / tools
per worker). Workers return structured results; orchestrator
aggregates. Differs from ReAct because workers operate in parallel
with isolated contexts.

components.name: Anti-pattern: Single-model dependency with no fallback
components.description: Pipeline hard-codes one model adapter. Production fails entirely when the model has an outage, rate-limit, region-block, deprecation, or quality regression. No graceful degradation, no second-best path.

components.name: Anti-pattern: GREP regex as a classifier
components.description: Stretching pattern-match rules to classify intent / sentiment / risk / topic when the signal is semantic, not lexical. Regex catches the surface form ('passport held') but misses synonyms ('document retained', 'ID locked away'). Becomes a Frankenstein of 200 alternations with corner cases that break each other.

components.name: LoRA merge + deploy (or hot-swap)
components.description: Two production deployment shapes for LoRA-trained models: (a) merge the LoRA delta into base weights once + serve as a single monolithic model — zero adapter overhead per request, no multi-tenant routing; or (b) keep the adapter separate + hot-swap per request — single base instance serves N task-specific tenants with cheap adapter switching. Pick by latency target + tenancy model.

components.name: RAG-Fusion
components.description:
Generate N rewrites of the user query, run retrieval for each, and
fuse the ranked lists via Reciprocal Rank Fusion. Outperforms single-
query retrieval when the user's wording differs from the corpus's.

components.name: Tree of Thoughts (ToT)
components.description:
At each reasoning step, propose N candidate "thoughts", value/vote
each, and expand the best ones via BFS or DFS. Backtrack on dead-ends.
Generalizes chain-of-thought from a single chain to a tree, allowing
exploration of multiple reasoning paths.

components.name: Anti-pattern: Un-cited extraction
components.description: Pipeline extracts fields from a document but the output does not include a (page, span) pointer back to the source. Downstream consumers cannot verify; mistakes propagate silently; the LLM's authoring style smooths over fabrications.

components.name: Personal preference layer
components.description: Treat individual user preferences as a first-class layer on top of any pipeline. The layer loads a preference knowledge pack at run-start, applies it to the persona system prompt, runs a personal-style GREP rule pack against generated output before delivery, and refuses to ship output that violates a hard-strictness preference. Preferences are stored locally, versioned, forkable.

components.name: K-anonymity + HMACed-key cross-organization aggregation
components.description:
Share *aggregated* signal across organizations without revealing
per-org provenance. Each org HMACs the key (broker ID, supplier
hash, address) with a hub-published salt so the same key produces
the same hash everywhere but cannot be reversed. Records are
bucketed by (hashed_key, category, time-window). Buckets with
`count < k` are dropped — small buckets could be re-identified
back to the contributing org. Surviving buckets are signed and
published.

Use whenever cross-org information sharing is required but
per-org confidentiality must be preserved: ESG bad-actor patterns,
AML mule networks, security threat indicators, fraud rings.

The k-anonymity threshold is the lever between strong-anonymity
(high k, slower signal) vs fast-pattern-surfacing (low k, higher
re-identification risk). Differential-privacy extensions add
calibrated noise to each bucket count for stronger guarantees.

components.name: QLoRA 4-bit fine-tune
components.description: Load base model in 4-bit NF4 quantization + freeze + insert LoRA adapters that train in BF16/FP16. Enables fine-tuning a 70B model on a single 48GB GPU (or 24GB with aggressive offload). De-quantize on-the-fly during forward pass; gradients flow only into LoRA adapters.

components.name: NERC CIP Compliance Analyst (Bulk Electric System cyber)
components.description:
Critical-infrastructure cybersecurity persona reviewing Bulk Electric
System (BES) cyber assets against NERC CIP-002 through CIP-014
standards: asset categorization, security management controls,
personnel & training, electronic security perimeters, physical
security, system security management, incident response, recovery
plans, configuration change management, information protection,
supply chain risk, physical security of substations.

components.name: Trust + Safety reviewer (platform moderation)
components.description: Persona for a platform Trust + Safety reviewer applying a documented content policy to user-generated content. Handles the standard T+S categories (CSAM, NCII, terrorist content, illegal goods, hate speech, harassment, self-harm, violent extremism, deceptive synthetic media, spam, child-grooming patterns). Refers, never decides on CSAM; immediately escalates per NCMEC reporting + applicable law.

components.name: Personal writing companion (loads user preferences)
components.description: A writing assistant persona that loads the user's personal style preferences before drafting. Strips disallowed idioms (em dashes, hedging adjectives, marketing-speak), enforces the user's preferred sentence length, vocabulary, and citation discipline.

components.name: Telco Compliance Officer (FCC Part 47 / CPNI / CALEA / TRAI)
components.description:
Telco compliance persona for reviewing carrier records for FCC
Part 47 regulatory obligations (Universal Service Fund, robocall
mitigation STIR/SHAKEN, network outage reporting), Customer
Proprietary Network Information (CPNI) protections under 47 USC
§222, CALEA lawful-intercept readiness, TRAI for India.

components.name: Fact Checker
components.description:
A careful fact-checker persona. Reasons about claims, evidence, and
the gap between them. Prefers primary sources and refuses to confirm
controversial claims without independent corroboration.

components.name: Security Engineer (CWE-cite-first AppSec reviewer)
components.description:
AppSec reviewer persona for source-code review. Citation-first:
every red-flag refers to a CWE (Common Weakness Enumeration) entry,
OWASP Top 10 category, or the specific MITRE ATT&CK technique the
pattern enables. Refuses to flag a code smell as a vulnerability
without a concrete CWE / OWASP reference.

components.name: Defense Acquisition Officer (DFARS / FAR / CMMC / counterfeit parts)
components.description:
US DoD acquisition compliance persona for reviewing defense
contracts + supplier flow-downs. Cite-first per DFARS clause /
FAR section / CMMC level / NIST SP 800-171 control / SAE AS5553
counterfeit-parts standard.

components.name: Translator + improver
components.description:
The second-most-cloned community persona. Translates input into
fluent English (or any target language) AND improves the original
for clarity, idiom, and register. Pairs naturally with
`rubric/bleu` / `rubric/meteor` evals.

components.name: ESG / Supply Chain Due Diligence Auditor (E + S + G)
components.description:
ESG auditor persona covering the full E + S + G dimensions of
supply-chain due diligence. Aligned to:

**EU**: CSDDD (Directive 2024/1760), CSRD ESRS standards
E1-E5/S1-S2, EUDR (Reg. 2023/1115), Conflict Minerals Regulation,
EU 2019/1937 (whistleblower).

**National (Europe)**: UK Modern Slavery Act §54, German LkSG,
France Loi Vigilance, Norway Åpenhetsloven, Switzerland CO Art.
964j, Netherlands Child Labour Due Diligence Law.

**North America / APAC**: California SB 657, US UFLPA, US Tariff
Act §307, Canada Bill S-211, Australia Modern Slavery Act, Japan
METI Guidelines.

**International**: ILO 11 forced-labor indicators, ILO core
conventions (29 / 87 / 98 / 100 / 105 / 111 / 138 / 182), UN
Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights, OECD MNE
Guidelines, ETI Base Code, ISO 37001 (anti-bribery).

Educational reference; real CSDDD compliance requires the lead
company's chief sustainability officer review, jurisdiction-
specific legal sign-off, and (per CSDDD Art. 26) board-level
oversight.

components.name: Clinical Reasoner
components.description:
A clinical-reasoning persona for decision-support assistants. Cites
guideline sections, flags red-flag symptoms, distinguishes
differential diagnoses, and refuses to give definitive diagnoses.
Educational / decision-support framing only — never a replacement
for a licensed clinician.

components.name: Inbox Zero Coach (triage + reply-draft + delegation)
components.description:
Email triage assistant that classifies each thread (do-now /
delegate / defer / drop) and drafts reply candidates using the
user's prior tone with each correspondent.

components.name: Transfer Pricing Counsel (OECD BEPS / IRC §482 / Pillar Two)
components.description:
International tax / transfer-pricing persona reviewing intercompany
transactions for arm's-length compliance against OECD Transfer
Pricing Guidelines, IRC §482, BEPS Action items (5/8-10/13/14),
Pillar One + Pillar Two (Global Minimum Tax 15%), and Country-by-
Country Reporting.

components.name: Commercial Space Launch Safety Officer (FAA Part 450 / NASA Range Safety)
components.description: Persona for licensed commercial space launch / reentry — FAA Part 450 + NASA-STD-8719.25 + ITAR/EAR USML XV / ECCN 9A515.

components.name: GxP Auditor (21-CFR-11 / EU GMP Annex 11 / ICH / ALCOA+)
components.description:
Pharmaceutical/biotech GxP auditor reviewing electronic records,
audit trails, and validated systems against 21-CFR-11, EU GMP
Annex 11, ICH Q9/Q10, ALCOA+ data-integrity principles, and EMA
GVP. Citation-first: every finding cites the exact regulation
section. Halts on uncovered PHI per HIPAA Safe Harbor.

components.name: Construction Safety Officer (OSHA 1926 / ANSI Z10 / Focus Four)
components.description:
Construction-site safety persona reviewing daily JHA / pre-task plans,
toolbox talks, near-miss reports, and OSHA 1926 compliance against
the Focus Four hazards (falls, struck-by, caught-in/between,
electrocution) plus PPE, scaffolding, excavation, fall-protection.

components.name: Trade Compliance Officer (HTS / ECCN / ITAR / OFAC)
components.description:
International-trade compliance persona reviewing product /
technology exports for: HTS classification (Harmonized Tariff
Schedule), ECCN classification (Export Control Classification
Number under EAR), ITAR jurisdiction (defense articles), OFAC
sanctions screening, end-use + end-user diligence, deemed exports.

components.name: Insurance Claims Adjuster (fraud-aware, NAIC-aligned)
components.description:
Insurance claims adjuster persona reviewing first-notice-of-loss
reports, repair invoices, medical bills, and supporting
documentation for legitimacy + fraud indicators. Aligned to NAIC
fraud red-flag taxonomy + Coalition Against Insurance Fraud
indicators. Citation-first: every adverse finding cites the
indicator family + policy section.

components.name: Government Benefits Adjudicator (SNAP / Medicaid / UI / SSI)
components.description:
Government benefits eligibility adjudicator. Applies the
programmatic rules (SNAP / Medicaid / Unemployment Insurance /
SSI / SSDI / TANF) to a beneficiary application, decides
eligibility, computes benefit amount, surfaces missing
documentation. Citation-first per CFR / state Medicaid plan /
USDA FNS handbook section.

components.name: Election Integrity Analyst (misinformation / deepfake / voter-roll)
components.description:
Election-integrity analyst persona for reviewing election-related
content for misinformation, AI-generated synthetic media (deepfakes),
coordinated inauthentic behavior, and voter-roll record validation.
Cite-first per EAC / CISA / DHS election-security guidance + ACT
on Election Integrity research methodology.

components.name: Academic Integrity Officer (plagiarism / AI-generated / citation fabrication)
components.description:
Higher-ed academic integrity persona reviewing student submissions
for plagiarism, AI-generated content, fabricated citations, and
honor-code violations. Cite-first — every flagged passage links to
the source evidence (TurnItIn-like similarity ID, AI-detector
confidence, citation-DB lookup).

components.name: Real Estate Due Diligence Analyst (title / environmental / structural / zoning)
components.description:
Commercial real-estate due-diligence persona reviewing property
acquisition packets: title search + chain of title + ALTA survey,
Phase I/II environmental site assessment (ESA), structural
inspection report, zoning compliance + variance history, rent
roll + lease abstracts. Cite-first with the source document.

components.name: Maritime Safety Officer (IMO SOLAS / MARPOL / ISM / STCW)
components.description:
Maritime-safety persona reviewing vessel inspection reports, port-
state control records, ISM Code SMS audits, MARPOL compliance,
ISPS port security, and STCW crew certifications. Cite-first per
IMO convention chapter + regulation.

components.name: Agriculture Compliance Officer (USDA / GAP / GlobalGAP / NOP organic)
components.description:
Ag-supply-chain compliance persona for farm + processor records:
USDA inspection / labeling (PACA + AMS), Good Agricultural
Practices (USDA GAP + GlobalGAP IFA), Organic certification
(NOP 7 CFR §205 + EU 2018/848), Federal Crop Insurance, USDA
GIPSA livestock, FDA FSMA (already covered in food_safety vertical).

components.name: Personal code reviewer (loads user preferences)
components.description: A code reviewer persona that loads the user's personal preferences before commenting. Encodes the reviewer's own style discipline (concise, no marketing-speak, no em dashes, prefer existing patterns over new abstractions, test changes before claiming success) instead of a generic vendor-default voice.

components.name: Threat Intelligence Analyst (MITRE ATT&CK + STIX/TAXII + IOC)
components.description:
CTI analyst persona for IOC verification, TTP mapping to MITRE
ATT&CK techniques, and threat-actor attribution analysis.
Citation-first: every assertion cites the ATT&CK technique
ID (T1234.xxx), CVE, or STIX object that grounds it. Never
asserts attribution without diamond-model evidence.

components.name: Radiologist (citation-first, specialty radiology)
components.description:
Specialization of `persona/clinical-reasoner` for radiology report
generation and review. Citation-first: every BI-RADS / Fleischner
/ ACR Appropriateness Criteria assertion is grounded with the
exact guideline section. Refuses to make incidental-findings
recommendations not backed by a guideline.

Educational reference — does not substitute for a board-certified
radiologist's report.

components.name: Gaming Integrity Officer (gambling AML / responsible gambling / licensing)
components.description:
Casino + sportsbook compliance persona for reviewing player records
for suspicious activity (BSA/AML for gaming under 31 CFR §1021),
responsible-gambling indicators (self-exclusion lists, problem-
gambling triggers), license obligations (state gaming commissions,
UK Gambling Commission, MGA Malta).

components.name: Personal Assistant (preference-aware, privacy-first)
components.description:
Personal AI assistant persona that reads user preferences BEFORE
taking action, never reveals private info to wrong audiences,
cites the user's stated rule when declining a request.

components.name: Local-first research tutor (CiteMind-style)
components.description: Persona for a privacy-preserving, source-grounded study assistant that answers strictly from the provided PDF + retrieved chunks + concept graph. Never invents facts beyond the document. Surfaces page numbers + section anchors in every answer.

components.name: Food Safety Officer (FDA FSMA / HACCP / GFSI)
components.description:
Food-safety persona reviewing supplier verification, HACCP plans,
recall traceability, and food-defense programs against FDA FSMA
rules (PCHF / FSVP / Produce Safety / Sanitary Transportation /
Intentional Adulteration) + HACCP principles + GFSI-recognized
schemes (SQF / BRCGS / FSSC 22000).

components.name: Water Utility Compliance Officer (SDWA / LCRR / AWIA)
components.description:
Drinking-water-utility compliance persona reviewing public-water-
system records against EPA Safe Drinking Water Act (SDWA), Lead and
Copper Rule Revisions (LCRR), Surface Water Treatment Rule, Disin-
fection Byproducts Rule, AWIA (America's Water Infrastructure Act)
cyber + physical-security requirements.

components.name: Cannabis Compliance Officer (State + IRS §280E + METRC + FinCEN)
components.description: Persona for licensed cannabis cultivation / manufacturing / dispensary / testing — state regulator + §280E + METRC + FinCEN 2014 guidance.

components.name: Math tutor
components.description:
Patient math-tutor persona that uses Socratic prompts and step-by-step
reasoning. Diagnoses what the student understands before introducing
new material. Useful baseline for `pipeline/cot-self-consistency` and
any reasoning-eval workflow.

components.name: Customer support agent
components.description:
Customer-support agent persona. Acknowledges the user's situation,
confirms the goal, proposes the next concrete step. Escalates to a
human if the issue cannot be solved in two messages or if it falls
into a sensitive category (refunds, legal, threats, policy
exceptions).

components.name: EEOC Hiring Officer (Title VII / ADA / ADEA / OFCCP)
components.description:
Hiring-compliance persona reviewing job postings, screening
questions, interview rubrics, and hiring decisions for
protected-class bias and disparate impact. Aligned to: Title VII
(race / color / religion / sex / national origin), ADA
(disability), ADEA (age 40+), GINA (genetic), Equal Pay Act,
PWFA (pregnancy), OFCCP Affirmative Action.

components.name: Linux terminal
components.description:
The single most-cloned community persona — model behaves like a Linux
terminal, returning only the would-be terminal output. Doubles as a
deterministic-IO eval (the same command should return the same shape
of output). Originally from f/awesome-chatgpt-prompts.

components.name: M&A Due Diligence Counsel (financial / legal / IP / customer / HR / tax)
components.description:
Senior M&A counsel persona reviewing target company DD across 6
classic streams: financial (audited financials, working capital,
EBITDA adjustments), legal (litigation, regulatory, change-of-
control restrictions), IP (patents / trademarks / copyrights /
trade secrets / open-source compliance), customer (concentration,
contracts, MAC), HR (key person, equity overhang, accrued PTO,
WARN), tax (NOLs, transfer pricing, sales/use, R&D credits).

components.name: RCRA Hazardous Waste Manager (40 CFR Parts 260-279 + e-Manifest + DOT HMR)
components.description: Persona for RCRA TSDF + LQG / SQG / VSQG generator + Universal Waste handler + DOT hazmat.

components.name: Privacy Officer (GDPR / CCPA / DSAR fulfillment)
components.description:
Privacy officer / DPO persona reviewing Data Subject Access
Requests (Art. 15), Right-to-Erasure (Art. 17), Right-to-
Rectification (Art. 16), Right-to-Portability (Art. 20),

(1399 votes, AIMO 2), mbmmurad QwQ-32B-preview (1097 votes),

components.name: Bill_info AI evaluation set (28 documents)
components.description:
The 28-document evaluation set used in Sviatoslav Grabovsky's
Bill_info AI (Gemma 4 Good Hackathon — Impact Track). Mixes:
 - Reddit-sourced real German Inkasso / Mahnung letters (from r/germany)
 - Redacted documents for refusal testing (7 documents)
 - Clear synthetic cases
 - Adversarial edge cases (e.g. synthetic Polish-IBAN scam letter)

Reported metrics on this set:
 - Documents tested: 28
 - Extraction success rate: 96% (27/28)
 - Overall field accuracy: 95.8%
 - Refusal accuracy on redacted documents: 100% (7/7)
 - Average latency (local): 8.5s

The single failure was a synthetic info-letter that triggered a
schema validation error — the safe failure mode. The validation
layer rejected malformed output rather than showing a plausible-
but-invalid analysis.

The original repository is the authoritative source — gold
standards + evaluation harness are reproducible via
`python -m eval.run_eval`.

required-for-jurisdiction sections rather than fail.

Includes 3 sample disclosure packs for testing:

Grade a radiology report against `rubric/radiology-report-quality-

pipeline:
 1. `processor/structured-to-prose` — flatten any structured input
 2. `processor/redact-pii-text` + `rule-pack/phi-hipaa-en` —
    strip PHI
 3. `rule-pack/grep-radiology-report-red-flags` — incidental
    findings + missing-RADS + Fleischner non-compliance heuristics
 4. RAG against `knowledge-pack/radiology-acrac-fleischner`
 5. `processor/llm-judge` against the rubric
 6. `processor/audit-trace-emitter` for record-keeping

Demonstrates the architecture's cross-vertical reusability: the
exact same harness/GREP/RAG/judge chain that grades ESG supplier
disclosures also grades radiology reports — only the rubric,
knowledge pack, and rule pack change.

components.name: MedLabel — photo-to-warning medicine safety pipeline
components.description:
Photograph of a medicine label → Gemma 4 vision extract (active
ingredients + dosage + strength + warnings) → drug-interaction
check against the user's current medication list → multilingual
user-language warning if a critical interaction or contraindication
is detected. Offline-first to work in low-connectivity settings.

Verified reference port of MedLabel by [unconfirmed author], shown
at the Gemma 4 Good Hackathon 2026 (companion to Bill_info AI in
the same hackathon's Impact Track — Digital Equity & Inclusivity /
Safety & Trust). Sourced from the project's public YouTube demo;
catalog port maintains the architectural shape but needs author
attribution + repo URL confirmation before claiming author co-
credit.

Implements the same 3 design patterns Bill_info AI publishes:
 - pattern/two-stage-extract-then-judge (extract fields → judge
   interaction risk)
 - pattern/critical-tier-output-override (severe interaction →
   "STOP, do not take, contact doctor" overrides anything else)
 - pattern/refuse-on-redacted (illegible label fields → null +
   "unreadable" warning rather than guessing dosage)
